title: Boston Fair Housing Evaluation RFP  EV00015524

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to specific records related to RFP EV00015524, issued by the Boston Planning Department and associated with a Fair Housing evaluation project, with procurement activity coordinated by Reuben Kantor.

To facilitate a prompt and efficient response, I am requesting the following records for the period June 1, 2025 through the present:

1. The executed contract(s), including any amendments, scope of work, or attachments, resulting from RFP EV00015524.
2. All invoices submitted by the selected vendor(s), along with any corresponding payment or approval records.
3. Any written progress reports, status updates, draft or final deliverables submitted by the vendor(s) to the Boston Planning Department.
4. Meeting agendas, minutes, summaries, or calendar entries documenting meetings between past and present Boston Planning Department staff and the vendor(s) concerning this project.
5. Meeting agendas, minutes, summaries, or calendar entries documenting meetings between past and present Boston Planning Department staff, members of Coalition for a Truly Affordable Boston. Armani White, Boston Tenant Coalition, Kathy Brown,  Lincoln Larmond, Margaret Turner, James Jenning, Matthew Lawlor, Barbara Parmenter, Aisha Miller, Jose Masso, Marvin Martin, Josh Wachman,  Abundant Housing, Elvira Moira, David Baron, Sherry Dong, Inya Ambrogi-Asaza, John Walkey, David Linhart, Travis Lee, L.Goodman, 
6. Email communications to or from Reuben Kantor and other Boston Planning Department staff that reference “EV00015524,” “fair housing,” or the selected vendor’s name, limited to correspondence concerning project status, milestones, deliverables, or performance.

To assist with timely production, I am willing to accept responsive records on a rolling or partial basis as they become available, rather than waiting for the entire request to be fulfilled.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon and release all reasonably segregable portions of responsive records.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RNPHD
